title: Logarithm Calculator (Any Base) | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate logarithms with any base instantly. Free, fast, and easy—find the exponent b that gives you x.
intro: Mathematics at every level—from school to university—relies on internationally validated standard formulas. Algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics, and trigonometry are the most consulted areas. A logarithm answers the question: "What exponent b, when raised to a power, gives me x?" That's log_b(x). Free and fast. All values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you up to date.
keyTakeaway: **Formula: log_b(x) = ln(x)/ln(b)**. Direct application with your input values. A logarithm finds the exponent: if b^y = x, then log_b(x) = y.

explanation:
**Mathematics** is a universal tool: algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics. Applied in engineering, science, finance, and everyday life.

## How to Calculate

A logarithm answers the question: "To what power must we raise the base b to get the number x?" This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unreasonable values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. All values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: A logarithm is an exponent. If b^y = x, then log_b(x) = y. It answers: 'To what power must I raise b to get x?'
The natural logarithm uses base e (≈2.71828). It's widely used in science, engineering, and calculus.
Base 10 logarithm, also called the common logarithm. Historically used in scientific calculations and logarithmic tables.
Base 2 logarithm, commonly used in computer science, information theory, and digital engineering.

faq.q: What are the main logarithm properties?
faq.a: Key properties: log(ab) = log(a) + log(b); log(a/b) = log(a) - log(b); log(a^n) = n·log(a).
The antilogarithm is the inverse operation. If log_b(x) = y, then b^y = x is the antilog.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration or sign-up required.
Results are based on internationally validated formulas and are approximate references. For critical applications, consult a professional.

faq.q: Is my data saved or sent to a server?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send or store any of your data on external servers.
